buggy_code:
fig, ax1 = plt.subplots()
ax2 = ax1.twinx()
ax1.plot(x, y1, 'b')
ax2.plot(x, y2, 'r')
ax1.set_ylabel('Temperature', color='b')
ax1.set_ylabel('Pressure', color='r')
task: Create dual y-axes: Temperature on left, Pressure on right
error_location: line 6: ax1.set_ylabel used again instead of ax2.set_ylabel
clarification_query: Which axis object should label the right y-axis (Pressure)?
corrected_code:
fig, ax1 = plt.subplots()
ax2 = ax1.twinx()
ax1.plot(x, y1, 'b')
ax2.plot(x, y2, 'r')
ax1.set_ylabel('Temperature', color='b')
ax2.set_ylabel('Pressure', color='r')
explanation: ax1.twinx() creates ax2 sharing the x-axis with a separate right y-axis. ax2.set_ylabel sets the right label.
